hyperedges.label: RepoMind Skill Pipeline: init -> prd -> query -> summary

nodes.label: repomind-query
nodes.rationale: Mandatory pre-step before any code Q&A, editing, or bug investigation. Routes via knowledge document frontmatter metadata (name/description/keywords) rather than a centralized index. Must be followed by repomind-summary gate.

nodes.label: repomind-summary
nodes.rationale: Mandatory synchronous summary gate required after every repomind-query, code change, bug fix, or verification. Determines whether new reusable knowledge (concepts/modules/troubles) should be written. Must complete before giving final answer.

nodes.label: Frontmatter Metadata Routing
nodes.rationale: Design decision: RepoMind routes queries via each knowledge document's own name/description frontmatter metadata, not through a centralized index.json or directory README. Module documents additionally maintain keywords. This distributes routing responsibility to individual documents.

nodes.label: Summary Gate
nodes.rationale: A synchronous blocking step that must run after every repomind-query or code change before giving the final answer. The gate decides whether knowledge base updates are needed; it cannot be skipped even if it ultimately determines no update is required.

nodes.label: Graphify Full Rebuild
nodes.rationale: During repomind-init, graphify must perform a full rebuild (not incremental) of the knowledge graph. Old graph results must not be reused. This ensures the graph accurately reflects the current codebase state.

nodes.label: Concept Confidence Levels
nodes.rationale: Three-tier classification for concept candidates during init: high (recurring in interfaces/services/models/configs/UX), medium (name looks like business capability but insufficient evidence), low (technical nouns, field names, internal tool names — discarded). Only high-confidence concepts are created; medium are noted as pending.

nodes.label: Knowledge Base Format Migration
nodes.rationale: Before any knowledge file read/write during init, 'repomind kb-migrate' must run to fix old formats: add name/description frontmatter to legacy docs, migrate reusable info from old index.json into module docs, delete obsolete centralized indexes and directory READMEs.

nodes.label: Module Candidate Classification
nodes.rationale: Three types: business modules (create/merge .repomind/modules/*.md), technical support modules (only create if they carry business entry points or cross-module business constraints), ignored directories (no module doc created).

hyperedges.label: RepoMind Skill Pipeline: init -> prd -> query -> summary
